_note: ILLUSTRATIVE MOCK KNOWLEDGE BASE - stands in for a live search against a real Help Centre. This is deliberately lightweight (a tagged list, matched by keyword overlap in plain Python, no API call) - the point being made is that retrieval-grounded drafting is a cheap, low-compute pattern. In production this file is replaced by a real search/retrieval call against the actual Help Centre; the pipeline code that consumes it does not change.

articles.title: Can't log in / password reset not arriving
articles.answer: Password reset emails can take a few minutes and are sometimes caught by spam filters - check there first. If it's been over 15 minutes with nothing in spam, the account may have an outdated recovery email on file, which needs a manual reset from the account owner or an admin.

articles.title: Two-factor code never arrives
articles.answer: 2FA codes over SMS can be delayed by the carrier, not our system - waiting 2-3 minutes usually resolves it. If it consistently fails, switching to an authenticator-app method in account settings is more reliable than SMS.

articles.title: SSO login certificate error
articles.answer: A certificate error on SSO login almost always means the identity provider's signing certificate was rotated and the new one hasn't been re-uploaded to the SSO configuration yet - an admin needs to re-upload the current certificate from the identity provider.

articles.title: Integration/webhook stopped firing
articles.answer: Integrations most commonly stop firing after an access token expires or a third-party app's permissions changed - reconnecting the integration from scratch in settings resolves the large majority of cases.

articles.title: Duplicate or unexpected charge
articles.answer: Duplicate charges are usually caused by a seat being added and removed within the same billing cycle before the change synced - check the invoice line items for two charges on the same seat and request a credit for the duplicate, which is issued automatically once confirmed.

articles.title: Refund process
articles.answer: Refunds for a billing error are issued automatically within 3-5 business days once confirmed. Refunds tied to a service issue (an outage or bug that affected usage) go through a short review first to confirm the impact window.

articles.title: Cancel a subscription
articles.answer: A subscription can be cancelled any time from account settings, effective at the end of the current billing cycle - access continues until then, and there's no early-cancellation fee on monthly plans.

articles.title: Close account
articles.answer: Closing an account stops all future billing at the end of the current cycle and retains historical workspace data for the legally required period before deletion. There's no early-cancellation fee on self-serve monthly plans; annual plans may have one - check the specific plan terms.

articles.title: Sync issue between desktop and web
articles.answer: A sync gap between desktop and web is almost always caused by the desktop app running an out-of-date local cache - signing out and back in on desktop forces a fresh sync and resolves it in most cases.

articles.title: Attachment upload limit lower than expected
articles.answer: Upload limits are set per-workspace, not per-plan, and sometimes an older workspace-level setting overrides the plan default - an admin can update the workspace file-size limit directly in workspace settings to match the plan's stated limit.
